strengths: ~20 years of continuous engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — meets the 15-year minimum with margin
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) signals an active pivot toward the role's core focus
Consistent community-builder record: open-source maintainer, conference speaker (FOSS4G), and multi-module workshop creator demonstrating the knowledge-sharing responsibility the role requires
concerns: AI-specific work begins in April 2025; no demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale prior to that — the role requires recognized expertise, not an emerging focus
Career arc is hands-on senior/staff IC, not director-level strategic advisor; résumé contains zero evidence of advising enterprise customers or partners on AI/cloud adoption strategy
Cloud depth is surface-level (AWS CDK within one pricing team, Kubernetes for a self-hosted NGO project) — insufficient for a role centered on cloud product strategy and platform differentiation
response.justification: The two highest-weighted competencies for this role — recognized AI expertise with demonstrated products at scale, and director-level strategic advisory experience — are both absent from Bogdan's résumé. His AI involvement is a single role starting April 2025 involving MCP tooling; the role explicitly seeks someone with an established AI product and platform track record. His career is consistently that of a craftsman IC (senior/staff developer), never a principal or director-level strategist shaping cloud or AI direction for enterprise customers. Cloud experience does not rise above team-level tooling choices. On the positive side, his 20-year polyglot engineering tenure clears the experience floor, and his recent direction toward agentic infrastructure and his open-source leadership are genuine signals of potential — but they do not close the gap to the seniority and AI depth this position demands. No advanced degree in an AI-related field further distances him from preferred qualifications. Score derived from meeting experience floor but failing on the two most critical competencies.
